Package org.areslib.subsystems.drive

Class TeleopDriveMath

java.lang.Object
org.areslib.subsystems.drive.TeleopDriveMath
public class TeleopDriveMath extends Object
Teleop drive math utilities for processing raw joystick inputs into smooth, predictable robot motion. Ported from MARSLib swerve drive stack.

Provides deadband filtering, exponential response curves, and angular input scaling for precision driving during teleop.

Mathematical References:

  • Polar Deadbands: "Doing Thumbstick Dead Zones Right"
  • Exponential Curves: f(x) = sgn(x) * |x|^k, where k is the sensitivity exponent.

  • Constructor Details

    • TeleopDriveMath

      public TeleopDriveMath()

  • Method Details

    • applyDeadband

      public static double applyDeadband(double value, double deadband)
      Applies a deadband to a joystick axis value. Values within the deadband are zeroed.
      Parameters:
      value - Raw joystick value (-1.0 to 1.0).
      deadband - Deadband threshold (typically 0.05 to 0.15).
      Returns:
      Filtered value with deadband applied and rescaled to full range.

    • applyExponentialCurve

      public static double applyExponentialCurve(double value, double exponent)
      Applies an exponential response curve to a joystick axis value. This gives fine control at low inputs and fast response at high inputs.
      Parameters:
      value - Input value (-1.0 to 1.0), typically after deadband.
      exponent - The exponent for the curve (1.0 = linear, 2.0 = quadratic, 3.0 = cubic).
      Returns:
      Curve-adjusted value preserving sign.

    • processJoystickInput

      public static double processJoystickInput(double rawInput, double deadband, double exponent, double maxSpeedMpsOrRadPerSec)
      Full joystick processing pipeline: deadband → exponential curve → max speed scaling.
      Parameters:
      rawInput - Raw joystick value (-1.0 to 1.0).
      deadband - Deadband threshold.
      exponent - Response curve exponent.
      maxSpeedMpsOrRadPerSec - Maximum output speed (m/s or rad/s).
      Returns:
      Processed speed value ready for ChassisSpeeds.

    • processJoystick2D

      public static double[] processJoystick2D(double rawX, double rawY, double deadband, double exponent, double maxSpeedMps)
      Processes 2D joystick inputs (X and Y) using polar deadband for consistent diagonal behavior.

      A polar deadband treats the joystick as a single 2D vector rather than two independent axes. This prevents the "cross-shaped" dead zone that independent axis deadbands create.

      Parameters:
      rawX - Raw X-axis joystick value.
      rawY - Raw Y-axis joystick value.
      deadband - Polar deadband radius.
      exponent - Response curve exponent applied to the magnitude.
      maxSpeedMps - Maximum output speed.
      Returns:
      A double array [processedX, processedY] ready for ChassisSpeeds.
